reportingPaths.title: Process lifecycle
reportingPaths.summary: Start with business processes, then follow instances and submission records.

reportingPaths.title: Form design to submitted values
reportingPaths.summary: Use form definitions and fields to orient submitted data tables before aggregating.

reportingPaths.title: Users, groups, and roles
reportingPaths.summary: Map user records through group membership and role assignment tables.

reportingPaths.title: Tasks, timers, and history
reportingPaths.summary: Use worker instance tables and history tables for queue and task-state reporting.

reportingQuestions.question: Which processes exist and how are they named?
reportingQuestions.guidance: Start with process definition tables, then inspect process instance tables before counting activity.

reportingQuestions.question: How many submissions exist by process or date?
reportingQuestions.guidance: Use submission and process tables, then validate the join path from exported foreign keys or the diagram.

reportingQuestions.question: Where are submitted field values stored?
reportingQuestions.guidance: Use form and field definitions to identify the value tables, then verify value column meaning before reporting.

reportingQuestions.question: Who can access or administer Forms items?
reportingQuestions.guidance: Start with users and group/role mapping tables, then confirm the meaning of flags and role identifiers.

generatedReportingExamples.title: List processes
generatedReportingExamples.note: Generated from columns exported for this version. Narrow the SELECT list further before operational use.

generatedReportingExamples.title: Show task records for status review
generatedReportingExamples.sql:
SELECT TOP (100)
  [status],
  [updated_by_snapshotid],
  [instance_id],
  [instance_type],
  [ref_instance_id]
FROM [dbo].[cf_bp_worker_instances]
WHERE [status] IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY [update_date] DESC;
generatedReportingExamples.note: Generated using status-like column status. Confirm the meaning of each status value before reporting.

generatedReportingExamples.title: List users
generatedReportingExamples.sql:
SELECT TOP (100)
  u.[username],
  u.[displayname],
  u.[email],
  u.[sid],
  u.[password]
FROM [dbo].[cf_users] AS u
ORDER BY 1;
generatedReportingExamples.note: User table exists; inspect exported foreign keys before adding group or role joins.
